pi_items.source: Task instructions and export_result.sh script explicitly define this output path.
export_result.sh explicitly zips the output shapefile components to this path.
Task instructions request 5x5 degrees. verifier.py checks that the median cell area is ~25.0 square degrees.
verifier.py checks for grid overlap against a target bounding box of [112, -45, 154, -10]. The actual dataset bbox for Australia is [113.34, -43.63, 153.57, -10.67].
verifier.py requires a feature count of at least 30 (30 <= count <= 120).
verifier.py explicitly allows up to 120 features (30 <= count <= 120).

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the real Natural Earth 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' dataset, specifically targeting the Australia feature (which has an actual bounding box of approximately 113.3°E to 153.6°E, 43.6°S to 10.7°S). The agent must generate a polygon grid shapefile saved to /home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/australia_grid.shp. This output is zipped to /tmp/australia_grid.zip by the environment. The grid cells must have an area of approximately 25 square degrees (5x5 degrees). The verifier accepts grids with 30 to 120 features and checks for spatial overlap against a bounding box of 112°E to 154°E, 45°S to 10°S.
